notes: One antigravity (agy) session and one partner-adapter session (claudecode) driven concurrently in the SAME cwd by run-cell-multi.sh, observed by a single --record daemon. The daemon labels each adapter independently (disjoint ProcessMatchers + transcript roots), so the workspace events.jsonl carries two distinct sessions — one per adapter — each with its own working->ready arc and no cross-contamination. Adapter-agnostic spec: agentA is whichever session enters working first, agentB the other; both cells (antigravity + claudecode) share this expected.jsonl and the same workspace events.jsonl (each cell differs only in which transcript it stores for the replay golden). Phases anchor on a WORKING transition (claudecode emits a born-ready proc-<PID> placeholder; anchoring on working skips it, and new_session on agentB skips agentA's already-matched session). No token/cost observations: Antigravity omits usage from the JSONL — only agent label + independent arcs are asserted.

text: First agent's turn drives ready -> working (binds agentA; anchoring on working skips any born-ready placeholder)
First agent's turn completes: working -> ready
The OTHER agent (a DISTINCT session, different adapter, same cwd) drives ready -> working independently (new_session proves it is not agentA)
Second agent's turn completes: working -> ready — two agents, one workspace, labeled independently with no cross-contamination
